description: Codebase exploration agent — cert project covering tool selection, MCP, context management, session management.

phases.title: Built-in tools & MCP augmentation
phases.milestone: Pick the right tool deliberately; MCP resource removes exploratory calls.
definition_of_done: State Grep/Glob/Read/Write/Edit and why for any task
Edit→Read+Write fallback when no unique anchor
MCP resource exposes catalog without exploratory calls

phases.title: Exploration workflow
phases.milestone: Architectural task in plan mode; Explore subagent keeps main context clean.
definition_of_done: Plan mode for architectural task; direct execution for implementation
Explore subagent returns summary; main context stays clean

phases.title: Long-session context
phases.milestone: Long session stays coherent via scratchpad + delegation + /compact; crash recovery works.
definition_of_done: Scratchpad + subagent delegation + /compact keep session coherent
Crash recovery via manifest works on resume

phases.title: Sessions & iterative refinement
phases.milestone: Resume named session, fork for two approaches, interview pattern before implementing.
definition_of_done: --resume and fork_session used correctly; resume vs fresh decision made
Interview pattern used before implementing in unfamiliar area

phases.title: Write up the answers
phases.milestone: Five exam questions answered in own words.
definition_of_done: All five questions answered in own words

exam_questions: Grep vs Glob — when each, with an example?
Edit failed on non-unique match — what is the reliable fallback?
Long session cites 'typical patterns' — what is happening and which techniques fix it?
When do you resume a session vs start fresh with a summary?
What is fork_session for, and how does it differ from --resume?
